tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

response.delta: Let me start by scraping the Effect blog to find the last 3 articles. I'll also check
what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

content.text: Let me start by scraping the Effect blog to find the last 3 articles. I'll also check what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking the available schemas in parallel.

Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3

</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">

    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

content.text: Let me start by scraping the Effect blog to find the last 3 articles. I'll also check what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking the available schemas in parallel.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking the available schemas in parallel.

            - paragraph [ref=e234]: Hi Effecters!
            - paragraph [ref=e235]:
              - text: Welcome back to This Week In Effect (
              - strong [ref=e236]: TWIE
              - text: ) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.
            - paragraph [ref=e237]: Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.
            - paragraph [ref=e238]: To get started, below you’ll find links to our documentation and our guide for installing Effect. Enjoy!
            - list [ref=e239]:
              - listitem [ref=e240]:
                - link "Effect Documentation" [ref=e241] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: /docs/getting-started/introduction/
              - listitem [ref=e242]:
                - link "Installing Effect" [ref=e243] [cursor=pointer]:
                  - /url: /docs/getting-started/installation/
            - paragraph [ref=e244]:
              - strong [ref=e245]: "Recent major updates:"
            - list [ref=e246]:
              - listitem [ref=e247]:
                - link "Effect v4 Beta" [ref=e248] [cursor=pointer]:

                - text: Release! 🚀
              - listitem [ref=e249]:

                - text: Release.
              - listitem [ref=e251]:
                - text: Durable workflows in TypeScript with
                - link "@effect/workflow" [ref=e252] [cursor=pointer]:

                  - code [ref=e253] [cursor=pointer]: "@effect/workflow"
                - text: "- currently in alpha."
              - listitem [ref=e254]:
                - text: 6450+ community members on
                - link "Discord" [ref=e255] [cursor=pointer]:

                - text: .
            - paragraph [ref=e256]
            - generic [ref=e257]:
              - heading "Technology" [level=2] [ref=e258]
              - link "Technology" [ref=e259] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#technology"

            - generic [ref=e263]:
              - heading "Effect v4 Beta updates" [level=3] [ref=e264]
              - link "Effect v4 Beta updates" [ref=e265] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#effect-v4-beta-updates"
                - img [ref=e267] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e269]:
              - text: The v4 beta closed out May with a strong week across HTTP security, reactivity, Schema fixes, and continued documentation quality improvements — here are the most notable changes that landed in
              - code [ref=e270]: effect-smol
              - text: .
            - list [ref=e271]:
              - listitem [ref=e272]:
                - paragraph [ref=e273]:
                  - strong [ref=e274]: HttpApi security
                  - text: ": Added"
                  - code [ref=e275]: HttpApiSecurity.http
                  - text: for passing custom HTTP authentication schemes, and added custom HTTP security scheme generation in the OpenAPI output. This enables scenarios beyond the standard bearer/basic patterns.
              - listitem [ref=e276]:
                - paragraph [ref=e277]:
                  - strong [ref=e278]: Schema fixes & additions
                  - text: ": Reintroduced"
                  - code [ref=e279]: .value
                  - text: "on"
                  - code [ref=e280]: Schema.Array
                  - text: and
                  - code [ref=e281]: Schema.NonEmptyArray
                  - text: for consistency with other collection wrappers (
                  - code [ref=e282]: Chunk
                  - text: ","
                  - code [ref=e283]: HashSet
                  - text: ", etc.), fixed"
                  - code [ref=e284]: catch*
                  - text: combinators erasing unhandled error types, fixed arbitrary constraints for exclusive
                  - code [ref=e285]: BigInt
                  - text: ","
                  - code [ref=e286]: Date
                  - text: ", and integer number bounds, and added a JSON Schema custom annotation passthrough option for advanced use cases."
              - listitem [ref=e287]:
                - paragraph [ref=e288]:
                  - strong [ref=e289]: Reactivity
                  - text: ": Fixed"
                  - code [ref=e290]: AtomRef
                  - text: notify listener resubscribe, ensuring listeners correctly reattach after notification cycles.
              - listitem [ref=e291]:
                - paragraph [ref=e292]:
                  - strong [ref=e293]: CLI
                  - text: ": Added default value support to integer and file"
                  - code [ref=e294]: Prompt
                  - text: types, making CLI prompts more ergonomic when a sensible default exists.
              - listitem [ref=e295]:
                - paragraph [ref=e296]:
                  - strong [ref=e297]: Entity & Workflow
                  - text: ": Made"
                  - code [ref=e298]: EntityResource
                  - text: lazy by default for better performance, and fixed workflow durable shard group routing.
              - listitem [ref=e299]:
                - paragraph [ref=e300]:
                  - strong [ref=e301]: OpenAI
                  - text: ": Handled missing"
                  - code [ref=e302]: output
                  - text: arrays in OpenAI responses, and improved WebSocket error status reporting.
              - listitem [ref=e303]:
                - paragraph [ref=e304]:
                  - strong [ref=e305]: Bug fixes & internals
                  - text: ": Fixed"
                  - code [ref=e306]: RequestResolver
                  - text: per-request instance leak (
                  - code [ref=e307]: pendingBatches
                  - text: ), fixed logger string formatter quoting, stopped
                  - code [ref=e308]: catch*
                  - text: combinators from erasing unhandled error types, and clarified that
                  - code [ref=e309]: Data.$is(tag)
                  - text: only checks the
                  - code [ref=e310]: _tag
                  - text: field. Also emitted minimal docs in generated barrels to reduce bundle overhead.
              - listitem [ref=e311]:
                - paragraph [ref=e312]:
                  - strong [ref=e313]: Documentation
                  - text: ": Continued the JSDoc quality push with improvements across"
                  - code [ref=e314]: Effect
                  - text: core APIs, a new
                  - code [ref=e315]: Schedule
                  - text: cookbook, and corrected misleading API docs.
            - paragraph [ref=e316]:
              - text: Full changelog in the
              - link "effect-smol repository" [ref=e317] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect-smol
              - text: .

            - paragraph [ref=e250]:
              - text: Effect v4 Beta launched February 18, 2026. Since then, the team has been shipping at a relentless pace inside the
              - link "effect-smol repository" [ref=e251] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect-smol
                - code [ref=e252] [cursor=pointer]: effect-smol
                - text: repository
              - text: .
            - paragraph [ref=e253]: "If you’ve been following the weekly This Week In Effect summaries, you’ve seen each piece land individually. This post pulls it all together: what v4 set out to change, what has landed since launch, and where the beta has been moving over the past few months."
            - paragraph [ref=e254]
            - separator [ref=e255]
            - paragraph [ref=e256]
            - paragraph [ref=e257]:
              - link "Effect v4 Beta" [ref=e258] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/
              - text: "is the most ambitious release we’ve made so far, with three major goals:"
            - list [ref=e259]:
              - listitem [ref=e260]: a rewritten runtime
              - listitem [ref=e261]: smaller bundles
              - listitem [ref=e262]: unified package system
            - paragraph [ref=e263]: The core fiber runtime was rewritten from scratch with lower memory overhead, faster execution, and simpler internals. Many core modules were also rebuilt with bundle size in mind; for example, a minimal program using Effect, Stream, and Schema dropped from roughly 70 kB in v3 to about 20 kB in v4.
            - paragraph [ref=e265]:
              - text: Effect v4 Beta also introduced unified versioning across the ecosystem. Instead of coordinating separate versions across Effect,
              - code [ref=e266]: "@effect/platform"
              - text: ","
              - code [ref=e267]: "@effect/sql"
              - text: ","
              - code [ref=e268]: "@effect/rpc"
              - text: ", and other packages, the v4 ecosystem now ships together under one version number. Many previously separate packages have also been consolidated into the core effect package, while platform-specific or provider-specific integrations remain separate."
            - paragraph [ref=e269]: Another important piece of the Effect v4 Beta is unstable modules. New functionality can now ship under effect/unstable/*, making it easier to experiment with new APIs inside the main package without committing to long-term stability too early.
            - paragraph [ref=e270]
            - generic [ref=e271]:
              - heading "What has landed since launch" [level=2] [ref=e272]
              - link "What has landed since launch" [ref=e273] [cursor=pointer]:

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

raw.description: Whether to return without compression (in PNG format). Default is false, which returns a JPEG image.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

content.text: Let me start by scraping the Effect blog to find the last 3 articles. I'll also check what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking the available schemas in parallel.

                  - code [ref=e285]: BigInt
                  - text: ","

            - paragraph [ref=e253]: "If you’ve been following the weekly This Week In Effect summaries, you’ve seen each piece land individually. This post pulls it all together: what v4 set out to change, what has landed since launch, and where the beta has been moving over the past few months."

            - paragraph [ref=e263]: The core fiber runtime was rewritten from scratch with lower memory overhead, faster execution, and simpler internals. Many core modules were also rebuilt with bundle size in mind; for example, a minimal program using Effect, Stream, and Schema dropped from roughly 70 kB in v3 to about 20 kB in v4.

            - paragraph [ref=e699]: "The launch set the foundation: a rewritten runtime, smaller bundles, unified versioning, a consolidated core package, and a path for experimental APIs."
            - paragraph [ref=e700]: "Since then, the work has been about carrying that foundation across the ecosystem: porting modules, refining APIs, smoothing migrations, improving docs, and tightening the developer experience."

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

content.text: Let me start by scraping the Effect blog to find the last 3 articles. I'll also check what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking the available schemas in parallel.

                - text: repository
              - text: .
            - paragraph [ref=e253]: "If you’ve been following the weekly This Week In Effect summaries, you’ve seen each piece land individually. This post pulls it all together: what v4 set out to change, what has landed since launch, and where the beta has been moving over the past few months."
            - paragraph [ref=e254]
            - separator [ref=e255]
            - paragraph [ref=e256]
            - paragraph [ref=e257]:
              - link "Effect v4 Beta" [ref=e258] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/
              - text: "is the most ambitious release we’ve made so far, with three major goals:"
            - list [ref=e259]:
              - listitem [ref=e260]: a rewritten runtime
              - listitem [ref=e261]: smaller bundles
              - listitem [ref=e262]: unified package system
            - paragraph [ref=e263]: The core fiber runtime was rewritten from scratch with lower memory overhead, faster execution, and simpler internals. Many core modules were also rebuilt with bundle size in mind; for example, a minimal program using Effect, Stream, and Schema dropped from roughly 70 kB in v3 to about 20 kB in v4.
            - paragraph [ref=e265]:
              - text: Effect v4 Beta also introduced unified versioning across the ecosystem. Instead of coordinating separate versions across Effect,
              - code [ref=e266]: "@effect/platform"
              - text: ","
              - code [ref=e267]: "@effect/sql"
              - text: ","
              - code [ref=e268]: "@effect/rpc"
              - text: ", and other packages, the v4 ecosystem now ships together under one version number. Many previously separate packages have also been consolidated into the core effect package, while platform-specific or provider-specific integrations remain separate."
            - paragraph [ref=e269]: Another important piece of the Effect v4 Beta is unstable modules. New functionality can now ship under effect/unstable/*, making it easier to experiment with new APIs inside the main package without committing to long-term stability too early.

- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You are able to connect and use a virtual browser with persistent session.
  Browser tools are provided via MCP.
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

<blueprint>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</blueprint>

Scrape effect blog at https://effect.website/blog and find the content of last 3 articles.
          Create/update Person object for each author.
          Create Markdown document for each article.

content.text: Let me start by scraping the Effect blog to find the last 3 articles. I'll also check what schemas are available for Person objects.
I'll start by navigating to the Effect blog and checking the available schemas in parallel.

              - heading "Effect v4 Beta updates" [level=3] [ref=e264]

                - /url: "#effect-v4-beta-updates"
                - img [ref=e267] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e269]:
              - text: The v4 beta closed out May with a strong week across HTTP security, reactivity, Schema fixes, and continued documentation quality improvements — here are the most notable changes that landed in
              - code [ref=e270]: effect-smol
              - text: .
            - list [ref=e271]:
              - listitem [ref=e272]:
                - paragraph [ref=e273]:
                  - strong [ref=e274]: HttpApi security
                  - text: ": Added"
                  - code [ref=e275]: HttpApiSecurity.http
                  - text: for passing custom HTTP authentication schemes, and added custom HTTP security scheme generation in the OpenAPI output. This enables scenarios beyond the standard bearer/basic patterns.
              - listitem [ref=e276]:
                - paragraph [ref=e277]:
                  - strong [ref=e278]: Schema fixes & additions
                  - text: ": Reintroduced"
                  - code [ref=e279]: .value
                  - text: "on"
                  - code [ref=e280]: Schema.Array
                  - text: and
                  - code [ref=e281]: Schema.NonEmptyArray
                  - text: for consistency with other collection wrappers (
                  - code [ref=e282]: Chunk
                  - text: ","
                  - code [ref=e283]: HashSet
                  - text: ", etc.), fixed"
                  - code [ref=e284]: catch*
                  - text: combinators erasing unhandled error types, fixed arbitrary constraints for exclusive
                  - code [ref=e285]: BigInt
                  - text: ","
                  - code [ref=e286]: Date
                  - text: ", and integer number bounds, and added a JSON Schema custom annotation passthrough option for advanced use cases."
              - listitem [ref=e287]:
                - paragraph [ref=e288]:
                  - strong [ref=e289]: Reactivity
                  - text: ": Fixed"
                  - code [ref=e290]: AtomRef
                  - text: notify listener resubscribe, ensuring listeners correctly reattach after notification cycles.
              - listitem [ref=e291]:
                - paragraph [ref=e292]:
                  - strong [ref=e293]: CLI
                  - text: ": Added default value support to integer and file"
                  - code [ref=e294]: Prompt
                  - text: types, making CLI prompts more ergonomic when a sensible default exists.
              - listitem [ref=e295]:
                - paragraph [ref=e296]:
                  - strong [ref=e297]: Entity & Workflow
                  - text: ": Made"
                  - code [ref=e298]: EntityResource
                  - text: lazy by default for better performance, and fixed workflow durable shard group routing.
              - listitem [ref=e299]:
                - paragraph [ref=e300]:
                  - strong [ref=e301]: OpenAI
                  - text: ": Handled missing"
                  - code [ref=e302]: output
                  - text: arrays in OpenAI responses, and improved WebSocket error status reporting.
              - listitem [ref=e303]:
                - paragraph [ref=e304]:
                  - strong [ref=e305]: Bug fixes & internals
                  - text: ": Fixed"
                  - code [ref=e306]: RequestResolver
                  - text: per-request instance leak (
                  - code [ref=e307]: pendingBatches
                  - text: ), fixed logger string formatter quoting, stopped
                  - code [ref=e308]: catch*
                  - text: combinators from erasing unhandled error types, and clarified that
                  - code [ref=e309]: Data.$is(tag)
                  - text: only checks the
                  - code [ref=e310]: _tag
                  - text: field. Also emitted minimal docs in generated barrels to reduce bundle overhead.
              - listitem [ref=e311]:
                - paragraph [ref=e312]:
                  - strong [ref=e313]: Documentation
                  - text: ": Continued the JSDoc quality push with improvements across"
                  - code [ref=e314]: Effect
                  - text: core APIs, a new
                  - code [ref=e315]: Schedule
                  - text: cookbook, and corrected misleading API docs.
            - paragraph [ref=e316]:
              - text: Full changelog in the
              - link "effect-smol repository" [ref=e317] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect-smol
              - text: .

            - paragraph [ref=e250]:
              - text: Effect v4 Beta launched February 18, 2026. Since then, the team has been shipping at a relentless pace inside the
              - link "effect-smol repository" [ref=e251] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect-smol
                - code [ref=e252] [cursor=pointer]: effect-smol
                - text: repository
              - text: .
            - paragraph [ref=e253]: "If you’ve been following the weekly This Week In Effect summaries, you’ve seen each piece land individually. This post pulls it all together: what v4 set out to change, what has landed since launch, and where the beta has been moving over the past few months."
            - paragraph [ref=e254]
            - separator [ref=e255]
            - paragraph [ref=e256]
            - paragraph [ref=e257]:
              - link "Effect v4 Beta" [ref=e258] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/
              - text: "is the most ambitious release we’ve made so far, with three major goals:"
            - list [ref=e259]:
              - listitem [ref=e260]: a rewritten runtime
              - listitem [ref=e261]: smaller bundles
              - listitem [ref=e262]: unified package system
            - paragraph [ref=e263]: The core fiber runtime was rewritten from scratch with lower memory overhead, faster execution, and simpler internals. Many core modules were also rebuilt with bundle size in mind; for example, a minimal program using Effect, Stream, and Schema dropped from roughly 70 kB in v3 to about 20 kB in v4.
            - paragraph [ref=e265]:
              - text: Effect v4 Beta also introduced unified versioning across the ecosystem. Instead of coordinating separate versions across Effect,
              - code [ref=e266]: "@effect/platform"
              - text: ","
              - code [ref=e267]: "@effect/sql"
              - text: ","
              - code [ref=e268]: "@effect/rpc"
              - text: ", and other packages, the v4 ecosystem now ships together under one version number. Many previously separate packages have also been consolidated into the core effect package, while platform-specific or provider-specific integrations remain separate."
            - paragraph [ref=e269]: Another important piece of the Effect v4 Beta is unstable modules. New functionality can now ship under effect/unstable/*, making it easier to experiment with new APIs inside the main package without committing to long-term stability too early.
            - paragraph [ref=e270]
            - generic [ref=e271]:

            - generic [ref=e263]:

            - generic [ref=e269]:
              - heading "Effect v4 Beta updates" [level=3] [ref=e270]
              - link "Effect v4 Beta updates" [ref=e271] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: "#effect-v4-beta-updates"
                - img [ref=e273] [cursor=pointer]
            - paragraph [ref=e275]:
              - text: This week, Effect v4 Beta got a major push on documentation quality, new stream and CLI primitives, and several notable API refinements — here are the most notable changes that landed in
              - code [ref=e276]: effect-smol
              - text: .
            - list [ref=e277]:
              - listitem [ref=e278]:
                - paragraph [ref=e279]:
                  - strong [ref=e280]: Documentation overhaul
                  - text: ": Standardized JSDoc example imports across the entire codebase, normalized category tags, validated JSDoc links in the new"
                  - code [ref=e281]: standard-jsdoc
                  - text: lint rule, vetted
                  - code [ref=e282]: "@since"
                  - text: tags between v3 and v4, and improved example titles and generated indexes. A new
                  - code [ref=e283]: standard-jsdoc
                  - text: ESLint rule is now enabled across the library.
              - listitem [ref=e284]:
                - paragraph [ref=e285]:
                  - strong [ref=e286]: New Stream APIs
                  - text: ": Added"
                  - code [ref=e287]: Stream.broadcastN
                  - text: for broadcasting to N downstream consumers, added
                  - code [ref=e288]: Schedule.tap
                  - text: for side-effecting on schedule steps, and fixed
                  - code [ref=e289]: Channel.decodeText
                  - text: to correctly handle UTF-8 chunk boundaries.
              - listitem [ref=e290]:
                - paragraph [ref=e291]:
                  - strong [ref=e292]: CLI additions
                  - text: ": Added"
                  - code [ref=e293]: Command.withHidden
                  - text: to hide subcommands from help output, added a hidden flag primitive, and used a predicate for
                  - code [ref=e294]: Argument.variadic
                  - text: validation.
              - listitem [ref=e295]:
                - paragraph [ref=e296]:
                  - strong [ref=e297]: Schema changes
                  - text: ": Changed"
                  - code [ref=e298]: Schema.asserts
                  - text: and
                  - code [ref=e299]: SchemaParser.asserts
                  - text: to assert a value directly with
                  - code [ref=e300]: asserts(schema, input)
                  - text: ", removing"
                  - code [ref=e301]: Schema.Codec.ToAsserts
                  - text: . Also moved previous API names to migration maps for smoother upgrading.
              - listitem [ref=e302]:
                - paragraph [ref=e303]:
                  - strong [ref=e304]: Platform
                  - text: ": Added a"
                  - code [ref=e305]: Crypto
                  - text: service to
                  - code [ref=e306]: "@effect/platform"
                  - text: ", exposing a cross-platform cryptography API."
              - listitem [ref=e307]:
                - paragraph [ref=e308]:
                  - strong [ref=e309]: Model
                  - text: ": Renamed"
                  - code [ref=e310]: Model.Generated
                  - text: to
                  - code [ref=e311]: Model.GeneratedByDb
                  - text: for clarity on database-generated fields.
              - listitem [ref=e312]:
                - paragraph [ref=e313]:
                  - strong [ref=e314]: Bug fixes
                  - text: ": Fixed"
                  - code [ref=e315]: TestClock
                  - text: layer Scope requirement, fixed IndexedDB bulk writes in transactions, fixed
                  - code [ref=e316]: RpcWorker
                  - text: Protocol service key, fixed stream scoped pulls to correctly provide scope, fixed
                  - code [ref=e317]: Channel.decodeText
                  - text: UTF-8 chunk boundary handling, and widened
                  - code [ref=e318]: OpenAIFile.expires_at
                  - text: and
                  - code [ref=e319]: status_details
                  - text: to accept
                  - code [ref=e320]: "null"
                  - text: .
              - listitem [ref=e321]:
                - paragraph [ref=e322]:
                  - strong [ref=e323]: SQL & Sharding
                  - text: ": Added support for"
                  - code [ref=e324]: .mts
                  - text: and
                  - code [ref=e325]: .mjs
                  - text: SQL migration files, and added
                  - code [ref=e326]: availableShardGroups
                  - text: to
                  - code [ref=e327]: ShardingConfig
                  - text: to prevent advisory lock conflicts.
            - paragraph [ref=e328]:
              - text: You can follow the full changelog directly in the
              - link "effect-smol repository" [ref=e329] [cursor=pointer]:
                - /url: https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect-smol
              - text: .

params.name: This Week in Effect - 2026-05-29
params.content:
# This Week in Effect - 2026-05-29

**Date:** May 29, 2026  
**Authors:** Davide Scognamiglio, Mirela Prifti, Michael Arnaldi  
**Tags:** This Week In Effect  
**Source:** https://effect.website/blog/this-week-in-effect/2026/05/29/

---

Hi Effecters!

Welcome back to This Week In Effect (**TWIE**) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.

Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.

**Recent major updates:**
- [Effect v4 Beta](https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/) Release! 🚀
- [Effect AI SDK](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/releases/tag/%40effect%2Fai%400.27.0) Release.
- Durable workflows in TypeScript with `@effect/workflow` - currently in alpha.
- 6450+ community members on Discord.

## Technology

### Effect v4 Beta updates

The v4 beta closed out May with a strong week across HTTP security, reactivity, Schema fixes, and continued documentation quality improvements — here are the most notable changes that landed in `effect-smol`.

- **HttpApi security**: Added `HttpApiSecurity.http` for passing custom HTTP authentication schemes, and added custom HTTP security scheme generation in the OpenAPI output.
- **Schema fixes & additions**: Reintroduced `.value` on `Schema.Array` and `Schema.NonEmptyArray` for consistency, fixed `catch*` combinators erasing unhandled error types, fixed arbitrary constraints for exclusive `BigInt`, `Date`, and integer number bounds, and added a JSON Schema custom annotation passthrough option.
- **Reactivity**: Fixed `AtomRef` notify listener resubscribe.
- **CLI**: Added default value support to integer and file `Prompt` types.
- **Entity & Workflow**: Made `EntityResource` lazy by default, fixed workflow durable shard group routing.
- **OpenAI**: Handled missing `output` arrays in OpenAI responses, improved WebSocket error status reporting.
- **Bug fixes & internals**: Fixed `RequestResolver` per-request instance leak, fixed logger string formatter quoting, stopped `catch*` combinators from erasing unhandled error types, clarified that `Data.$is(tag)` only checks the `_tag` field, emitted minimal docs in generated barrels.
- **Documentation**: Continued the JSDoc quality push with improvements across `Effect` core APIs, a new `Schedule` cookbook, and corrected misleading API docs.

## Community Events

Effect Vienna 🇦🇹 and Effect Miami 🇺🇸 meetups happened this week, hosted by Sentry & The LAB Miami. The upcoming Effect Milan 🇮🇹 meetup is happening on June 11, hosted by Datapizza.

## Community Highlights

- Dillon Mulroy shared tips on how he writes Effect (Effect.gen vs .pipe) and noted Effect crossed **15M weekly downloads** 📈
- Tim Suchanek highlighted the legibility of Effect code as a superpower for reviewing AI-generated code
- "One Effect to Rule them all" - Mayflower article about adopting Effect as their standard framework (German 🇩🇪)
- GPT 5.5 one-shotted Effect v3 to v4 migration in 60k LOC project
- Kyle Mistele credits Effect for solving one of their hardest bugs
- Foldkit making waves after Office Hours
- effect-agents, TUI Components, Pivit, livetrace, Veya - new community projects

## Cause & Effect Podcast

Adam Rankin, CTO at Warp, joined Johannes Schickling to talk about using Effect for reliable payment & payroll systems.

## Closing Notes

That's all for this week. Thank you for being a vital part of our community.

*Effect Community Team*

params.name: Effect v4 Beta: February–May Recap
params.content:
# Effect v4 Beta: February–May Recap

**Date:** May 28, 2026  
**Authors:** Davide Scognamiglio, Mirela Prifti  
**Tags:** Releases, Effect v4 Beta Recaps  
**Source:** https://effect.website/blog/effect-v4beta-launch-to-may-recap/

---

Effect v4 Beta launched February 18, 2026. Since then, the team has been shipping at a relentless pace inside the `effect-smol` repository.

Effect v4 Beta is the most ambitious release so far, with three major goals:
- A rewritten runtime
- Smaller bundles
- Unified package system

The core fiber runtime was rewritten from scratch with lower memory overhead, faster execution, and simpler internals. A minimal program using Effect, Stream, and Schema dropped from roughly 70 kB in v3 to about 20 kB in v4.

Effect v4 Beta also introduced unified versioning across the ecosystem and unstable modules under `effect/unstable/*`.

## What has landed since launch

### Schema
- API consolidation and renaming (`makeUnsafe` → `make`, `ExtendableClass` merged into `Class`)
- New constructors: `Schema.makeOption`, `Schema.asClass`, `Schema.annotateEncoded`, `Schema.DurationFromString`, `Config.literals`, various `*FromString` schemas
- Context-aware defaults for schema decoding
- Class improvements (void constructors, `MissingSelfGeneric` error, `ErrorClass`/`TaggedErrorClass` toString)
- Concurrent schema property processing
- JSON Schema output improvements (`compactEnums`, custom annotations)
- Type ergonomics improvements
- Bug fixes for `catch*` combinators, arbitrary constraints
- `Model.Generated` renamed to `Model.GeneratedByDb`

### IndexedDB
- First-class browser persistence merged in early April
- Streaming and offset support, compound indexes, `.reverse()`, `rebuild` API, improved transaction API, `.reactive` queries, `Model.Class` as IDB schemas, cached base query builders

### HTTP & HttpApi
- `HttpApi.gen` code generation, reworked `HttpApiClient`, `HttpApiTest` module
- `HttpApiSecurity.http` for custom authentication schemes
- Static file server, `expireCookie`, `Headers.removeMany`, `HttpClient.withRateLimiter`
- Various bug fixes

### RPC
- Deferred responses, `RpcGroup.omit`, `Rpc.custom`, `RpcWorker` Protocol, `rpc ConnectionHooks`
- Bug fixes for entity proxy, finalizer request IDs

### AI
- OpenAI: custom model/request properties, reasoning support, WebSocket error reporting
- Fixed streaming and tool output persistence
- Fixes for OpenRouter and Amazon Bedrock providers

### Workflow & Durable Execution
- Fixed workflow failures being squashed by suspension interrupts
- Isolated workflow suspension, fixed `Latch.release`, parent pointer forwarding
- `DurableQueue` ported from v3 to v4

### SQL
- New `@effect/sql-pglite` package
- `Sql.unique`, `KeyValueStore.layerSql`, `makeMsgPack`, `.mts`/`.mjs` migration files
- PostgreSQL: dedicated PG connection for `LISTEN`, `pg_notify`

### Platform & Runtime
- `Effect.abortSignal`, `Socket.make`, `Effectable` module, `Effect.acquireDisposable`, `Effect.firstSuccessOf`, `Crypto` service, `Layer.suspend`
- Type inference fixes with `NoInfer`
- Bug fixes for `forkScoped`, SIGINT listener, `TestClock`, `formatJson`, `Schedule.fixed`, `RequestResolver`, `AtomRef`

### Stream & Scheduling
- `Stream.broadcastN`, `Schedule.tap`, `Stream.timeoutOrElse`, `Stream` service accessors
- Bug fixes for `Channel.decodeText`, `Stream.groupedWithin`, `Effect.repeat`

### CLI
- `Command.withHidden`, hidden flag primitive, `Argument.variadic` validation, `Command.withSharedFlags`, default values for Prompt types
- Various bug fixes

### Context (formerly ServiceMap)
- `ServiceMap` renamed back to `Context`, `EventLog` identity base64 encoding

### Sharding & Cluster
- `availableShardGroups`, Kubernetes support, `ShardId` cleanup

### Developer Experience & Documentation
- Standardized JSDoc, `standard-jsdoc` ESLint rule, `@since` tag vetting, `Schedule` cookbook
- Removed `Effect.Yieldable`, `Schema.Codec.ToAsserts`, `dtslint` → `typetest`
- Migration guides: [v3 to v4](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect-smol/blob/main/MIGRATION.md) and [Schema v4](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect-smol/blob/main/packages/effect/SCHEMA.md)

## The Effect v4 Beta has been moving fast

Effect v4 is still in beta, so breaking changes may occur. Install the beta, build something real, and share what works, what breaks, and what feels off.

```
npm install effect@beta
```

Happy Effecting. 🚀

params.name: This Week in Effect - 2026-05-22
params.content:
# This Week in Effect - 2026-05-22

**Date:** May 22, 2026  
**Authors:** Davide Scognamiglio, Mirela Prifti, Michael Arnaldi  
**Tags:** This Week In Effect  
**Source:** https://effect.website/blog/this-week-in-effect/2026/05/22/

---

Hi Effecters!

Welcome back to This Week In Effect (**TWIE**) - your weekly update of the latest developments in the Effect community and ecosystem.

Effect is a powerful TypeScript library that helps developers build complex, synchronous, and asynchronous programs. One key feature that sets Effect apart is how it leverages structured concurrency to provide features such as async cancellation and safe resource management, making it easier to build robust, scalable, and efficient programs.

**Recent major updates:**
- [Effect v4 Beta](https://effect.website/blog/releases/effect/40-beta/) Release! 🚀
- [Effect AI SDK](https://github.com/Effect-TS/effect/releases/tag/%40effect%2Fai%400.27.0) Release.
- Durable workflows in TypeScript with `@effect/workflow` - currently in alpha.
- 6410+ community members on Discord.

## Technology

### Effect v4 Beta updates

This week, Effect v4 Beta got a major push on documentation quality, new stream and CLI primitives, and several notable API refinements.

- **Documentation overhaul**: Standardized JSDoc example imports across the entire codebase, normalized category tags, validated JSDoc links in the new `standard-jsdoc` lint rule, vetted `@since` tags between v3 and v4, and improved example titles and generated indexes.
- **New Stream APIs**: Added `Stream.broadcastN` for broadcasting to N downstream consumers, added `Schedule.tap` for side-effecting on schedule steps, and fixed `Channel.decodeText` to correctly handle UTF-8 chunk boundaries.
- **CLI additions**: Added `Command.withHidden` to hide subcommands from help output, added a hidden flag primitive, and used a predicate for `Argument.variadic` validation.
- **Schema changes**: Changed `Schema.asserts` and `SchemaParser.asserts` to assert a value directly with `asserts(schema, input)`, removing `Schema.Codec.ToAsserts`. Also moved previous API names to migration maps.
- **Platform**: Added a `Crypto` service to `@effect/platform`, exposing a cross-platform cryptography API.
- **Model**: Renamed `Model.Generated` to `Model.GeneratedByDb` for clarity on database-generated fields.
- **Bug fixes**: Fixed `TestClock` layer Scope requirement, fixed IndexedDB bulk writes in transactions, fixed `RpcWorker` Protocol service key, fixed stream scoped pulls, fixed `Channel.decodeText` UTF-8 chunk boundary handling, and widened `OpenAIFile.expires_at` and `status_details` to accept `null`.
- **SQL & Sharding**: Added support for `.mts` and `.mjs` SQL migration files, and added `availableShardGroups` to `ShardingConfig` to prevent advisory lock conflicts.

### Effect AI
- Fix(ai-amazon-bedrock): strip thinking from additionalModelRequestFields when using generateObject

### Effect Workflow
- Fix(workflow): forward parent pointer when spawning a child with discard:true

## Community Events

- Jan Peer Stöcklmair speaking at **Effect Vienna** meetup on May 27
- Dax speaking at **Effect Miami** meetup on May 28
- **Effect Milan** 🇮🇹 on June 11, hosted by Datapizza
- Possible **Effect San Francisco** meetup in the works

## Community Highlights

- Matt Pocock: "There is literally no better AX for building backend stuff in TypeScript. Once you see it through that lens it's addictive"
- Dillon Mulroy: Effect + Alchemy 💯
- Kyle Mistele on incremental Effect adoption: "wrap a flaky vendor API in an effect, add bounded exponential backoff + jitter — no other opinions required"
- Will King: "Even Codex gets it."
- Adam: "I am creating a new app and I am using Effect. Please respect my privacy at this time." (475 likes)
- Jamie Turner (Convex): "Wait till you try Effect plus Convex together."
- Community resources: effect-cf (Cloudflare Workers), effect-boxes v0.15.0, effect-coffee-shop, effect-hatchet, Cortex (vector DB ORM)

## Effect Content Update

- Foldkit, the frontend framework built on Effect | Effect Office Hours 29 🔥
- Why Alchemy is Cloudflare-first: CDK, Terraform & Tooling Q&A
- Reviewing a persistence package repository
- Next Era of TypeScript | Effect.ts × AI by Lucas Barake
- Neverthrow vs Effect.ts by Lucas Barake

## Cause & Effect Podcast

Adam Rankin, CTO at Warp, joined Johannes Schickling to talk about using Effect for reliable payment & payroll systems.

## Closing Notes

That's all for this week. Thank you for being a vital part of our community.

*Effect Community Team*
